hero.title: Interactive Quizzes Made Simple
hero.subtitle: Create engaging quizzes, collect real-time responses, and visualize results instantly. Perfect for classrooms, meetings, and events.

features.title: Why Choose Swaya.me?
features.createQuizzesDesc: Build multiple-choice and word cloud questions in minutes. No technical skills required.
features.liveParticipationDesc: Participants join instantly with a simple code. No login required for audience members.
features.realTimeResultsDesc: See responses as they come in. Perfect for instant feedback and engagement.
features.wordCloudsDesc: Collect open-ended responses and visualize them as beautiful word clouds.

cta.title: Ready to Get Started?
cta.subtitle: Join educators, trainers, and presenters using Swaya.me to make their sessions more interactive.

about.title: About Swaya.me
about.subtitle: A live audience engagement platform built for educators, trainers, and presenters who want real interaction — not just passive viewing.
about.whatBody1: Swaya.me is a real-time interactive quiz and polling platform. Hosts create sessions in minutes; audiences join instantly using a 6-digit code — no app download, no account, no friction.
about.whatBody2: Create Online Quizzes, Polls, and Tests with six question types: MCQ, Word Cloud, Scale (1–5), Single-line, Paragraph, and One-word — all with rich text support. Results are visualised live as participants respond, with leaderboard scoring and time-based tiebreaking. Full session reports can be exported as PDF, Word, PowerPoint, or Excel.
about.pricingBody: Swaya.me is completely free during its Beta phase. Paid pricing plans with higher limits and additional features will be introduced after Beta ends. All existing users will be notified before any changes take effect.
about.betaBody: Swaya.me is currently in Beta. The platform is actively developed and new features are released regularly. We welcome your feedback — use the in-app feedback option or contact us directly.
about.contactBody: We'd love to hear from you — feedback, bug reports, or business enquiries.
about.useCase1Desc: Run formative assessments, knowledge checks, and interactive revision sessions in classrooms of any size.
about.useCase2Desc: Keep workshop and training audiences engaged with live polls, quizzes, and word clouds.
about.useCase3Desc: Turn passive conference or webinar audiences into active participants with zero setup friction.
about.useCase4Desc: Run quick team pulse checks, icebreakers, or knowledge-sharing sessions with your organisation.
about.feature1: Six question types: MCQ, Word Cloud, Scale (1–5), Single-line, Paragraph, and One-word — with rich text editing
about.feature2: Three modes: Online Quiz (auto-scored), Online Poll, and Test (with negative marking and LLM semantic grading)
about.feature4: Anonymous participation — join via 6-digit code or QR; no app or account needed
about.feature5: Live result visualisation and leaderboard with time-based tiebreaking
about.feature6: Export results as PDF, Word, PowerPoint, or Excel
about.feature7: Offline Poll mode — share polls without hosting a live session
about.feature8: Sign in with Google or email — quick, no friction
about.feature9: 11-language interface: English, Hindi, Tamil, Telugu, Kannada, Bengali, Gujarati, Spanish, French, German, Russian
about.feature10: Tier-based plans for individuals, teams, and enterprises (post Beta)

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: At Swaya.me, your privacy is important to us. This policy explains what information we collect, how we use it, and your rights in relation to it.
privacy.s1Body1: When you create an account, we collect your name, email address, and organisation name. We store quiz content you create, including questions, options, and session results.
privacy.s1Body2: Participants join sessions anonymously. We collect only the display name you optionally provide and your quiz responses. No login or personal account is required.
privacy.s1Body3: We collect standard server logs including IP addresses, browser type, and page interaction data for platform security and performance monitoring. We also track language preference selections to improve localisation.
privacy.s2Intro: We use the information we collect for the following purposes:
privacy.s2li1: To operate and deliver the Swaya.me quiz and polling platform
privacy.s2li2: To authenticate registered users and manage their accounts
privacy.s2li3: To display quiz results and session reports to hosts
privacy.s2li4: To monitor platform performance, security, and reliability
privacy.s2li5: To improve and develop new features based on usage patterns
privacy.s2Note: We do not sell, rent, or share your personal data with third parties for marketing purposes.
privacy.s3Body: We use browser session storage (not persistent cookies) to maintain your session as an audience participant during a quiz. Registered users use JWT tokens stored in localStorage for authentication. No third-party tracking cookies are set.
privacy.s4Body1: Registered user accounts and associated quiz data are retained for as long as your account is active. You may request deletion of your account and data by contacting
privacy.s4Body2: Anonymous participant responses are retained with the session record for the duration the host maintains their quiz history.
privacy.s5Body: We use industry-standard security measures including HTTPS encryption for all data in transit and hashed passwords for stored credentials. While we take reasonable precautions, no internet service can guarantee absolute security.
privacy.s6Body: Swaya.me is not directed at children under 13. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children under 13. If you believe a child has provided personal information, please contact us for removal.
privacy.s7Body: Swaya.me does not use third-party advertising services or sell data. We use infrastructure services (server hosting, database) that process data on our behalf under standard data processing agreements.
privacy.s8Body: Depending on your jurisdiction, you may have rights to access, correct, or delete your personal data. To exercise these rights, please contact us at
privacy.s9Body: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. When we do, we will update the 'Last updated' date at the top of this page. For significant changes, we will notify registered users by email.
privacy.s10Body: For any privacy-related questions or requests, please contact us at

help.heroSubtitle: Everything you need to get started with Swaya.me
help.heroNoteEnd: button — click it for a screenshot and detailed instructions.
help.navHostDesc: Create and run interactive sessions for your audience.
help.navParticipantDesc: Join a live session with a code — no account needed.
help.navQuizVsPollDesc: Not sure which to use? See the difference with real examples.
help.navExportDesc: Download session results as PDF, Word, PowerPoint, or Excel.
help.hostsIntro: Follow these steps to create and run your first quiz. Click
help.hostsIntroEnd: on any step to see a screenshot and detailed instructions.
help.audienceIntro: Joining a session is quick and requires no account. Click
help.audienceIntroEnd: on any step for a screenshot and detailed instructions.
help.questionTypesIntro: Swaya.me supports five question types. Click any type for a full description.
help.quizVsPollTitle: Quiz vs Poll — When to Use What
help.quizVsPollIntro2: . Both use the same join-code workflow and look identical to participants when joining — but they are fundamentally different in one key way:
help.quizVsPollKeyDiff: only a Quiz has a leaderboard
help.quizVsPollIntro3: . Scores are calculated from correct answers, and ties are broken by speed.
help.leaderboardTitle: The Leaderboard — the defining Quiz feature
help.leaderboardIntro: The leaderboard is what makes a
help.leaderboardIntroEnd: . It appears live on the host's screen and on each participant's device after every question.
help.leaderboardRank1Text: Each MCQ question is worth 1 point. Only the correct option earns the point.
help.leaderboardRank2Text: If two participants have the same score, whoever answered correctly faster is ranked higher. So answering quickly matters.
help.leaderboardRank3Text: The leaderboard refreshes after every question. Participants can see their own rank highlighted on their device.
help.leaderboardRank4Text: The host can choose to show or hide the leaderboard from participants at any time using the toggle on the session control screen.
help.pollNoLeaderboardText: Since there are no correct answers, there is nothing to rank. All poll responses are shown as charts, clouds, or averages — but no participant is ranked above another.
help.howToCreateTitle: How to create a Quiz or Poll
help.howToCreateIntro: From your Dashboard, you'll see two buttons:
help.howToCreateEnd: . Click the one that matches your goal — the builder opens in the right mode automatically.
help.howToCreateDrilldownAlt: Dashboard showing New Quiz and Create Poll buttons
howToCreateTips: Log in and go to your Dashboard.
Click "New Quiz" to open the quiz builder in Quiz mode (MCQ with correct answers).
Click "Create Poll" to open the builder in Poll mode (all question types, no correct answers).
Give your quiz or poll a title and description.
Click "Add Question" to start adding questions — the available types depend on the mode.
When done, click "Publish" to make it ready to run.
help.exampleQuizIntro: In a Quiz, every MCQ question needs a correct answer marked. Participants are scored on correctness and speed. Use these as inspiration:
help.examplePollIntro: In a Poll, there are no right or wrong answers. Mix and match question types to suit your session. Click any type below to see example questions:
help.quickRuleQuizMid: with ranked scores and a winner? → use a
help.exportIntro: After a session ends, you can download the full results in four formats — PDF, Word, PowerPoint, and Excel. Each format is designed for a different use case.
help.exportAccessTitle: How to access the Export button
help.exportAccessMid: , then find the session you want to export. Each ended session has an
help.exportAccessEnd: button on the right — click it to choose a format.
help.exportHistoryAlt: Quiz history page showing session rows with Export buttons
exportHistoryTips: Log in and go to your Dashboard.
Find the quiz and click on it to open its detail page.
Click the "History" tab to see all past and active sessions.
Each row shows a session with its status (ENDED, LIVE, etc.).
For any ENDED session, click the Export (download) button on the right side of the row.
A dropdown appears with the four format options — click the one you want.
Your browser will download the file immediately.
help.exportDropdownAlt: Export dropdown showing PDF, Word, PowerPoint and Excel options
help.exportWhichPdf: — share with stakeholders who just need to read the results
help.exportWhichWord: — insert results into an existing report or document
help.exportWhichPowerPoint: — present results live in your next meeting
help.exportWhichExcel: — dig into the raw data, filter, sort, and analyse
help.contactText: If you have a question that isn't answered here, reach out to us at

hostSteps.title: Create an account
hostSteps.description: Register at swaya.me, verify your email address.

hostSteps.title: Build your quiz
hostSteps.description: Dashboard → New Quiz → Add questions (MCQ, Word Cloud, Scale, Single-line, Paragraph) → Publish.

hostSteps.title: Start a session
hostSteps.description: Open your quiz → Start Session → share the 6-character join code (or QR code) with your audience.

hostSteps.title: Run the session
hostSteps.description: Advance questions one by one, watch live results, end the session when done.

hostSteps.title: Export results
hostSteps.description: Download a full report as PDF, DOCX, PPTX, or XLSX from the session results page.

hostDetails.screenshotAlt: Swaya.me home page with Sign Up button
tips: Click "Sign Up" on the home page or go to swaya.me/register.
Enter your name, email address, and choose a password.
Check your inbox for a verification email and click the link to activate your account.
Once verified, log in — you land on your Dashboard.
From the Dashboard, click "New Quiz" (top right).
Give your quiz a title and optional description.
Click "Add Question" to add your first question — choose a type from the dropdown (MCQ, Word Cloud, Scale, etc.).
For MCQ questions, fill in all 4 options and tick the correct answer.
You can upload an image for any question using the image icon.
When finished, click "Publish" to make the quiz ready to run.

hostDetails.screenshotAlt: Session start screen showing join code and QR code
tips: From your Dashboard, click the quiz you want to run.
Click "Start Session" — a join code (6 characters) appears on screen.
A QR code is also shown — participants can scan it instead of typing the code.
Display the join code on a screen/projector, or read it out loud.
Participants go to swaya.me and type this code to join.

hostDetails.screenshotAlt: Host session control screen with live results
tips: Once participants have joined, click "Start Question" to show the first question.
Watch live results update on your screen as participants answer.
When ready, click "Next Question" to advance.
You can see how many participants have answered before moving on.
Click "End Session" when all questions are done.
After ending a session, you are taken to the results page automatically.
You can also reach it from Dashboard → click your quiz → Session History.
Click "Export" to download results as PDF, Word (DOCX), PowerPoint (PPTX), or Excel (XLSX).
Each export includes per-question breakdowns and participant scores.

audienceSteps.title: Go to swaya.me
audienceSteps.description: No account needed. Open swaya.me in any modern browser on your phone or computer.

audienceSteps.title: Enter the join code
audienceSteps.description: Type the 6-character code from your host (or scan the QR code). Enter a display name when prompted.

audienceSteps.title: Answer questions
audienceSteps.description: Questions appear as the host advances — tap an option, type a word, drag a slider, or type your response.

audienceSteps.title: See live results
audienceSteps.description: After each question, live results are shown to everyone in the session.

audienceDetails.screenshotAlt: Swaya.me home page with join code field
tips: Open any web browser on your phone or computer.
Go to swaya.me — no app download needed.
You do not need to create an account or sign in.
You will see a box on the home page asking for a join code — you can type it directly there.

audienceDetails.screenshotAlt: Join page with code entered and name field
tips: Your host will display a 6-character code on a screen — type it into the join box.
Alternatively, scan the QR code shown by your host using your phone camera.
You will be asked to enter a display name — this is the name other participants will see.
Tap "Join" to enter the session.
Questions appear on your screen as soon as the host advances them.
For Multiple Choice: tap the option you think is correct.
For Word Cloud: type a word or short phrase and tap "Submit".
For Scale questions: drag the slider to your chosen value.
For text questions: type your response and tap "Submit".
Answer quickly — some question types are scored by speed.
After you submit an answer, a waiting screen appears.
Once the host advances, results for that question are shown to everyone.
For MCQ, you can see how many people picked each option and whether you were correct.
For Word Cloud, watch all submitted words appear as a live cloud.

questionTypes.detail: The host sets 4 answer options and marks one as correct. Participants tap their choice. Scoring is based on correctness and, optionally, speed. Results show a bar chart of votes after the question closes.

questionTypes.summary: Participants type a word — shown as a live cloud.
questionTypes.detail: Everyone types a word or short phrase. All responses appear together as a word cloud, with more frequent answers shown larger. There is no "correct" answer — it is great for brainstorming and open opinions.

questionTypes.summary: Rate something on a numeric scale.
questionTypes.detail: Participants drag a slider to a number (e.g. 1–10). Results show the average score and distribution. Use it for sentiment checks, confidence ratings, or polling a range of opinions.
Participants type a short text response. Good for names, quick answers, or any brief input. Responses are listed for the host to review.
Like Single-line but designed for longer answers. Use for open-ended feedback, detailed explanations, or essay-style responses.

quizVsPollComparison.quiz: ✅ Live leaderboard — participants ranked by score, ties broken by speed
quizVsPollComparison.poll: ❌ No leaderboard — all responses are equal, no ranking

quizVsPollComparison.quiz: +1 point per correct MCQ answer; faster = higher rank on a tie
quizVsPollComparison.poll: Not scored — opinions and feelings have no right answer

quizVsPollComparison.quiz: Test knowledge — find out who knows what
quizVsPollComparison.poll: Gather opinions — find out what people think

quizVsPollComparison.quiz: You mark one correct option per MCQ question
quizVsPollComparison.poll: No correct answer — all options are valid choices
MCQ, Word Cloud, Scale, Single-line, Paragraph

questions.text: Which planet is closest to the Sun?
questions.tip: Mercury is the correct answer. Set option B as "Mercury" and mark it correct.

questions.text: What is the chemical symbol for Gold?
questions.tip: Au (from the Latin "Aurum"). Mark option C as correct.
HyperText Markup Language. Mark option A as correct.

questions.text: Which of these is NOT a programming language?
questions.tip: "Cobra" is not a real programming language. Mark option B as correct.

questions.text: How did you hear about this event?
questions.tip: Leave "correct answer" blank — all options are equally valid.

questions.text: Which session topic are you most excited about?
questions.tip: Great for live audience preference checks before scheduling.

questions.text: In one word — how do you feel about the project so far?
questions.tip: All responses appear as a live word cloud. Bigger = more common.

questions.text: What word best describes our company culture?
How confident are you in the new process? (1 = Not at all, 10 = Very confident)
questions.tip: Participants drag a slider. Results show the average and spread.

questions.text: How likely are you to recommend this event to a colleague? (1–10)
questions.tip: This is essentially a live NPS (Net Promoter Score) question.

questions.text: What's one thing you'd like us to improve before the next session?
questions.tip: Participants type as much as they like. Responses are collected for you to review.

questions.text: Describe the biggest challenge your team is facing right now.
questions.tip: Good for retrospectives and problem-surfacing workshops.

questions.text: What is your favourite team-building activity?
questions.tip: Short answers, perfect for quick check-ins.

questions.text: Name one thing you learned today.
questions.tip: Great as a closing question to consolidate learning.

exportFormats.label: PDF
exportFormats.useCase: Share results as a polished report — email it or print it.
includes: Quiz / poll title, date, and total participants
Each question with the full results breakdown
Bar chart images for MCQ questions
Word cloud images for Word Cloud questions
Leaderboard table (Quiz mode only)

exportFormats.label: Word
exportFormats.useCase: Edit the results in Microsoft Word or Google Docs.
includes: Quiz / poll summary and metadata
Full question-by-question breakdown with answer counts
Charts and word cloud images embedded as images
Leaderboard table (Quiz mode only)
Editable text — ideal for inserting into reports or slide decks

exportFormats.label: PowerPoint
exportFormats.useCase: Present results directly in a slideshow.
includes: One slide per question with results visualised
Charts and word cloud images on each slide
A summary slide with participant count and session info
Leaderboard slide (Quiz mode only)
Editable in PowerPoint, Keynote, or Google Slides

exportFormats.label: Excel
exportFormats.useCase: Analyse raw data, build pivot tables, or import into another tool.
includes: Multiple sheets: Summary, Question Results, Raw Answers, Leaderboard
Raw answer data — one row per participant per question
Timestamps and time-taken values for each answer
Leaderboard sheet with scores and response times (Quiz mode only)
Ideal for deep analysis or feeding into other systems

faqItems.question: Do I need an account to join a session?
faqItems.answer: No. Audience members never need to create an account. Just go to swaya.me, enter the join code your host gives you, and pick a display name — that's it.

faqItems.question: How many questions can I add to a quiz?
faqItems.answer: The number of questions depends on your plan tier (FREE, BASIC, PRO, ENTERPRISE). Check your Dashboard for your current limits, or contact us to upgrade.

faqItems.question: Can I add images to questions?
faqItems.answer: Yes. In the quiz builder, each question has an optional image upload field. Supported formats include JPG, PNG, and GIF.

faqItems.question: How do I share my join code with the audience?
faqItems.answer: When you start a session, a join code and QR code are shown on the session control screen. You can display this on a projector, share a screenshot, or read the code aloud. Participants can also scan the QR code directly.

faqItems.question: Where can I see the results after a session?
faqItems.answer: Go to your Dashboard, find the quiz, and click on the session history. Full results are available there and can be exported as PDF, Word, PowerPoint, or Excel.

faqItems.question: What browsers are supported?
faqItems.answer: Swaya.me works on all modern browsers — Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge — on both desktop and mobile. We recommend keeping your browser up to date for the best experience.

faqItems.question: Is my data private?
faqItems.answer: Yes. Please read our Privacy Policy for full details on how we collect, store, and protect your data.

recentUpdates.title: Recent Product Updates
recentUpdates.subtitle: These sections were recently updated and translated for all supported languages.
items: Authentication pages now use localized text for email verification, forgot password, and reset password states.
Admin tools now use localized labels and validation messages for user form, feedback filters, tier management, and platform quiz filters.
Quiz builder now uses localized text for Single Line, Paragraph, and Scale question labels and expected-answer summaries.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: By accessing or using Swaya.me, you agree to these Terms of Service. Please read them carefully before using the platform.
terms.s1Body1: Swaya.me is a live audience engagement and quiz platform. It allows registered hosts to create and run interactive sessions, and allows audience members to participate anonymously via a join code.
terms.s1Body2: The platform is currently in Beta. Features, limits, and pricing may change during the Beta period. We will notify users of significant changes before they take effect.
terms.s2Body: To create a host account, you must be at least 13 years of age. Audience participation does not require registration and has no age restriction beyond what the session host determines appropriate for their audience.
terms.s3Intro: When using Swaya.me, you agree not to:
terms.s3li1: Post or transmit content that is unlawful, harmful, abusive, defamatory, obscene, or otherwise objectionable
terms.s3li3: Attempt to gain unauthorised access to other accounts or platform systems
terms.s3li4: Use the platform to distribute spam, malware, or phishing content
terms.s3li5: Interfere with or disrupt the platform's services or infrastructure
terms.s3li6: Scrape or harvest data from the platform without authorisation
terms.s3Note: We reserve the right to suspend or terminate accounts that violate these terms.
terms.s4Body1: You retain ownership of quiz content you create on Swaya.me. By using the platform, you grant us a limited licence to store, display, and process your content solely to deliver the service to you.
terms.s4Body2: You are responsible for ensuring your quiz content complies with applicable laws and does not infringe third-party rights.
terms.s5Body: Platform-provided quiz templates are made available for your use as a host. You may use, copy, and adapt these templates to create your own quizzes. We retain ownership of the original templates.
terms.s6Body: We aim to provide a reliable service, but we do not guarantee uninterrupted availability. Swaya.me may be unavailable from time to time for maintenance or due to factors outside our control. We will endeavour to notify users in advance of planned downtime.
terms.s7Body1: To the maximum extent permitted by law, Swaya.me and its operators are not liable for any indirect, incidental, consequential, or punitive damages arising from your use of the platform.
terms.s7Body2: Our total liability to you for any claim arising from use of the platform shall not exceed the amount you have paid us in the twelve months preceding the claim (or zero if you are on a free plan).
terms.s8Body: Swaya.me and its logo are owned by Chakrix. The platform software, design, and branding are protected by applicable intellectual property laws. You may not copy, modify, or distribute our platform software or branding without prior written permission.
terms.s9Body: We may update these Terms of Service from time to time. We will notify registered users by email before significant changes take effect. Continued use of the platform after changes constitutes acceptance of the updated terms.
terms.s10Body: These Terms of Service are governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India. Any disputes shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of India.
terms.s11Body: If you have any questions about these Terms of Service, please contact us at

ai.topicPlaceholder: e.g. Solar System, World War II, Python basics
ai.noQuestionsGenerated: No questions were generated. Try refining your prompt.
ai.generationFailed: Generation failed. Please check your prompt and try again.
ai.promptHint: Be specific: include the topic, difficulty, audience, any constraints, and the style of questions you want.
ai.promptPlaceholder:
Examples:
• Generate questions about Python decorators for senior developers. Focus on real-world use cases and edge cases. Avoid theoretical definitions.
• Create 10 questions on the French Revolution for high school students — cover causes, key events, and important figures. Medium difficulty.
• Write questions on Indian constitutional amendments since 2000. Questions should be fact-based and suitable for a government exam prep quiz.

offlinePoll.typeInfo: No live host needed. Share a link — audience responds at their own pace. Results accumulate over time.

exam.saveExamFirst: Please save the test before adding questions
exam.resultsEmailPlaceholder: Email to notify when test closes
exam.emailInvalid: Please enter a valid email address
exam.otpSent: A 6-digit OTP has been sent to {{email}}
exam.otpRateLimited: Too many OTP requests. Please wait 10 minutes.
exam.submitConfirm: Are you sure you want to submit your test?
exam.alreadyAttempted: You have already attempted this test.
exam.examUpcoming: This test has not started yet.
exam.typeInfo: Timed, self-paced exam. Auto-scored with +/− points. Participants receive a rank and score at the end. Accessed via a link.
exam.timeLimitWarning: You have {{minutes}} minutes total from when you start.
exam.perQuestionTimerWarning: Each question has a time limit — it auto-advances when the timer runs out. You cannot go back to expired questions.
exam.requireEmailHint: Participants must enter their name and email, receive a one-time code, and verify it before the test opens. Their email is recorded against the result.
exam.saveSettingsHint: Saves schedule, scoring, and security settings in one click.
exam.liveEditBannerDesc: Schedule, scoring, and security settings can be updated and take effect immediately. Questions are locked — deactivate the exam to add or edit questions.
exam.questionsLockedNotice: Questions are locked while the exam is active. Click "Deactivate Exam" in the top bar to edit questions.
exam.aiAnalysisUnavailable: Analysis is available once at least one participant has submitted.

appFeedback.modalSubtitle: Help us improve Swaya.me — your feedback goes directly to our team.
appFeedback.placeholder: Tell us what worked well, what can improve, or report a bug...
appFeedback.errorMessage: Failed to submit feedback. Please try again.

tooltip.themeToggle: Switch between light and dark mode
tooltip.useTemplate: Start from a ready-made quiz — edit and launch in minutes
tooltip.makeTemplate: Save as a reusable template for your workspace
tooltip.quizHistory: View past session results and participant scores
tooltip.startSession: Launch a live session — share the join code with your audience
tooltip.publishQuiz: Make this quiz ready to run. You can still edit it after publishing.
tooltip.unpublishQuiz: Move back to draft to make edits
tooltip.copyShareLink: Copy the link to share directly with your audience
tooltip.questionPoints: Points awarded for a correct answer
tooltip.maxTime: Auto-advances after this many seconds. Leave empty for no limit.
tooltip.negativePoints: Points deducted for a wrong answer. Set to 0 to disable.
tooltip.expectedAnswer: Model answer — Swaya uses AI to mark by meaning, not exact wording
tooltip.richTextToggleQuestion: Switch to rich text to add bold, tables, code, and colour
tooltip.richTextToggleOptions: Format answer options with bold, italic, and other styles
tooltip.scaleCorrectAnswer: The scale value (1–5) that will be marked as correct
tooltip.revealAnswer: Show participants which answer was correct
tooltip.sessionCode: Share this code — participants go to swaya.me and enter it to join
tooltip.stopQuiz: End the live session. Results are saved and available in History.
tooltip.emptyStateSubtitle: Choose the type that fits your use case
tooltip.emptyStateQuizDesc: Live Quiz — run with an audience in real time
tooltip.emptyStatePollDesc: Live Poll — gather instant audience responses
tooltip.emptyStateOfflinePollDesc: Offline Poll — share a link, collect responses anytime
tooltip.emptyStateExamDesc: Test / Exam — timed, auto-scored, shareable via link

promo.tagline: Create your own polls & quizzes for free on Swaya.me
promo.bookmarkTip: Press Ctrl+D (or ⌘D on Mac) to bookmark this page

warning.title: This is a Proctored Examination
warning.subtitle: Read the following carefully before you proceed
warning.webcamNotice: Your webcam is being recorded and monitored live for the entire duration of this exam. Ensure your face is clearly visible, well-lit, and centred on camera at all times.
warning.violationsTitle: The following actions will be flagged as violations:
warning.lockMessage_one: After {{count}} violation, your exam will be permanently locked
warning.lockMessage_other: After {{count}} violations, your exam will be permanently locked
warning.autoSubmit: and your answers submitted automatically. You will not be able to re-enter.
warning.noAutoSubmit: . You will not be able to continue or re-enter.
warning.allLogged: All violations are logged and reviewed by the exam host.
warning.checkboxLabel: I have read and understood the above rules. I agree to take this exam honestly and accept that any violation may result in disqualification.

rules.tab_switch_detect: Switching to another tab, window, or application
rules.copy_paste_block: Copying or pasting any text during the exam
rules.right_click_block: Right-clicking anywhere on the exam page
rules.fullscreen_enforce: Exiting fullscreen or minimising the browser
rules.multi_tab_detect: Opening multiple browser tabs or windows
rules.bot_signal_detect: Using automation tools, scripts, or browser extensions that tamper with exam controls
rules.webcam_monitoring: Looking away from the screen, leaving camera frame, or allowing another person to appear on camera

overlay.violationMessage: A proctoring rule was triggered. Please follow the exam guidelines.
overlay.finalWarningMessage: This is your final warning. One more violation will lock your session.

lock.title: Session Locked
lock.adminMessage: Your session has been locked. Please contact the exam administrator.
lock.violationMessage: A proctoring violation was detected. Your session has been locked.

webcam.deniedMessage: This exam requires webcam access to proceed. Please allow camera access in your browser and reload the page.

fullscreen.required: This exam requires fullscreen mode. Click below to continue.
fullscreen.blockedDesc: This exam requires fullscreen mode. If your browser blocked fullscreen, check for extensions that prevent it, then click Retry.

report.title: Integrity Report
report.notEnabled: Proctoring is not enabled for this exam.
report.notEnabledDesc: Enable proctoring in the quiz settings to start tracking participant integrity.
report.enabledNoData: Proctoring is enabled — no participant data yet.
report.enabledNoDataDesc: Integrity data will appear here once participants start the exam through the proctored flow.

settings.title: Security & Proctoring
settings.enableHint: When disabled, no monitoring is active and participants have zero overhead.
settings.noRules: No rules available for your plan.
settings.examOnly: Proctoring is available for Exam and Offline Poll types only.
settings.silentHint: Silent — participant does not see a warning when this rule triggers
settings.webcamNotice: Webcam is enabled — participants who deny camera access will not be able to start this exam.

preset.standardDesc: Adds copy-paste blocking, multi-tab detection, and bot signal checks
preset.maximumDesc: All available rules for your plan are enabled

escalation.lockLabel: Lock exam after ___ warnings to participant
escalation.lockHint: Participant receives {{warnings}} warning(s), then is permanently locked on the {{lockAt}}th violation
escalation.autoSubmitLabel: Auto-submit answers when exam is locked
escalation.autoSubmitHint: When enabled, the participant's answers at the time of lock are submitted automatically
escalation.summary: With these settings: participant gets {{warnings}} warning(s), then locked on violation #{{lockAt}}
escalation.autoSubmitOn: Auto-submit is ON — their answers at the time of lock will be submitted automatically.
escalation.autoSubmitOff: Auto-submit is OFF — a locked participant's answers will not be submitted unless they manually submitted before being locked.

identityCapture.title: Identity Verification
identityCapture.instruction: Position your face clearly in the frame, then click Capture.
identityCapture.verified: Identity verified. You're ready to begin.
